infobox.Guest stars:: Kurt Fuller David Koechner Michael Daingerfield Michael McGlone Allie Bertram Zahf Paroo Byron Noble Shaw Madson

sections.heading: Intro
sections.text: "Office Space" is the eleventh episode of Season Seven . When Gus inadvertently tampers with a crime scene at his office, Shawn must find the murderer before Gus is arrested.

sections.heading: Contents
sections.text: 1 Plot Summary 2 Quotes 3 Trivia 4 Gallery 5 Digital Releases

sections.heading: Plot Summary
sections.text:
Shawn gets a middle-of-the-night visit from a frantic Gus telling him that he "screwed up bad". Gus says that he finally stood up to his horrible boss, Dorian Creech ( Michael Daingerfield ), and gave him his letter of resignation with the some threatening words written at the bottom. Before Shawn can congratulate Gus on his uncharacteristic assertiveness, Gus drops a bomb and explains that he later returned to the office to withdraw his resignation but found Creech stabbed to death in his office, clutching the note Gus had written to him earlier. Gus explains that he panicked, grabbed the note from Creech's dead hands and ate it. But then he started to choke on it, and things got out of control. Gus explains that he accidentally contaminated the crime scene and made it look like he was the one who killed Creech. Shawn instructs Gus to calm down and that they will head to the office and have a look.
At the office, Shawn sees the damage and is instantly worried; it's worse than he thought it was going to be. They both start to panic, one thing leads to another, and Shawn ends up with a bloody nose. He sneezes, getting blood all over the already tainted crime scene. Now, they are faced with having to clean up the whole crime scene or risk being arrested for Creech's murder. They do so, then decide to enlist the help of Henry .
The next morning, Creech's office has been cordoned off as a crime scene. The security guard at Central Coast Pharmaceuticals , Leslie Sally ( David Koechner ), tells Lassiter that he himself is going to head up part of the investigation. Sally informs Lassiter that the liner in the trash can is missing and points out that Creech is grasping something in his hand — part of Gus' note. Following his surprisingly acute detecting skills, Sally follows the path of a leaky trash bag to the parking garage. There, they find a piece of evidence — a coffee thermos Shawn brought from Juliet 's house and accidentally left there the night before. Since Juliet's fingerprints are on the mug, Shawn knows that he has to tell her the truth about what's going on so she doesn't get investigated for Creech's murder either. Juliet is beyond peeved, but she agrees to slow down the testing at the lab while Shawn and Gus find the real killer.
The following day, the office has gathered for a memorial. There, we meet the Senior VP from headquarters, Mitch Murray ( Michael McGlone ), Finch ( Zahf Paroo ), Creech's biggest kiss-butt employee, and Neil Nebersol ( Byron Noble ), a disgruntled employee who says he fantasized about killing Creech every day. Next, Shawn and Gus get a call from Woody saying that he found some incriminating evidence on the body – the smell of cocoa butter lotion and Axe body spray. Woody knows that Gus wears cocoa butter lotion and that Shawn wears Axe body spray, so he asks the boys to explain themselves. Shawn and Gus reassure Woody that they didn't kill Creech, but Woody admits he is terrible at keeping secrets, and allows them to tie him up at the Psych office until they have found the real killer.
Shawn and Gus go to question Creech's assistant, Helen Blaine ( Allie Bertram ). Helen tells them that she squirted eye drops in his coffee on the morning of his death because Creech had treated her poorly. When she went into his office later that night to retrieve a forgotten file, she found him lying dead on the floor, and therefore thought that it was the eye drops that poisoned him. Shawn and Gus know she isn't the killer because Creech died from stab wounds, not from illness. They tell Helen she's innocent and she's relieved.
Subsequently, Sally is running his own investigation. He gets handwriting samples from each employee, including Gus, and begins working on restoring the hard drives of the security camera outside Creech's office. Furthermore, he does some digging and also finds out about Gus' verbal confrontation with Creech on the morning of his death. Mitch confronts Gus, but instead of coming down on him, he offers him the job of branch Vice President. Sally seems suspicious of Gus and alerts him about the hard drives, but soon aligns himself with Mitch and decides to rule Gus out as a suspect due to his new position.
Later, Shawn and Gus tail Finch through the parking garage, but lose him after they hide behind a car. As they run through the garage looking for Finch, Sally approaches them and says he knows it was them who tampered with the crime scene. Sally found out when he discovered the bag of evidence they threw out a few blocks away, and the handwriting results point at Gus being the culprit. But before Sally can alert the cops, he starts foaming at the mouth and drops dead. Someone poisoned him. Shawn glances through the plastic bag of evidence Sally found and discovers two pieces of evidence that tell him who the real killer is.
Lassiter receives results from the lab and knows that Shawn and Gus tampered with the evidence at the scene. Shawn and Gus still need to prove their innocence in full, so they run from Lassiter and his assisting officers in order to do so. They head directly to Gus' office to breakdown the case in front of his co-workers. Shawn addresses the crowd and tells them that it was Mitch Murray who murdered Creech. Creech found out that Mitch was cooking the books and inflating profits for every branch of the company, and so Creech was blackmailing him and trying to make a play for Mitch's job at headquarters. Mitch killed Creech to keep him quiet, and when he found out that Sally was onto him, Mitch killed him, too, by poisoning his coffee. When confronted with the evidence, Mitch confesses, adding that he promoted Gus because he didn't think Gus was sharp enough to find evidence of the financial wrongdoings. Gus' promotion is short-lived.
Just as Lassiter is about to come down hard on Shawn and Gus for messing with a crime scene and tampering with evidence, Juliet arrests Mitch for the murder of Creech, and Lassiter lets the guys off with a warning – for now.

sections.heading: Quotes
sections.text:
Shawn: I think we should call the police and let the chips fall where they may. Gus: You're talking like a real white guy right now, Shawn. Brothers don't get the benefit of the doubt. I will not rot in a cell. You said we were in this together. Shawn: Come on, man. You know I'm gonna bake you a cake with a gun in it.
Mitch: Sally here has uncovered the details of a public confrontation you had with Mr. Creech yesterday. Gus: [ laughs nervously ] I don't know if I'd call it a confrontation. Mitch: Oh no? Gus: [ tries to play it off as nothing ] No. Mitch: You told him, and I quote, "Say hello to Beelzebub after you fly first-class to the fire pits of Hell, you black-hearted baby girl bastard." Gus: Hmm, I don't think those were my exact words.
Shawn: We are out of time. Lassie's about to have the print results. Jules is trying to stall him by pretending to be carrying Starfish Spencer. Gus: Starfish who? Shawn: My firstborn Starfish, named after the graceful and stylish sea creature. Gus: Oh. I like. I like it a lot.

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text: The episode was named after the 1999 comedy Office Space , and its signature red Swingline stapler makes an appearance on Gus's desk after his promotion. There are numerous references to Glengarry Glen Ross : Mitch Murray closely resembles GGR's Blake, who is sent to the office by the company's owners Mitch and Murray. Leslie Sally having Gus write "A-B-C" when he wants a handwriting sample is a reference to Blake's "Always Be Closing" acronym, and Mitch's DROS acronym (Deceased-Recovery-Opportunity-Smile) is a reference to Blake's AIDA acronym (Attention-Interest-Decision-Action). Shawn tells the staff that first prize for "this month's sales contest" is a Cadillac El Dorado and second prize is a set of steak knives, the same prizes offered by Blake in his speech. Mitch tells Shawn "No one even knows your name" and Shawn replies "Suck it – that's my name," a bowdlerized rendition of the moment when Blake is asked "What's your name?" and he replies, "F*** you – that's my name!" A similar bowdlerization occurs when Mitch mentions "brass stones" as opposed to Blake's "brass balls". The episode likely also took inspiration from the 1980 comedy 9 to 5 , as both are in an office setting, feature mistreated secretaries who believe they've killed their boss by putting something in his coffee, and include pivotal moments related to a higher-up being exposed for a crime. A pineapple appears on one of the scratch and sniff stickers in Mitch's briefcase. The song Finch sings at Creech's memorial is a take on "Time After Time" by Cyndi Lauper. Shawn answers Woody 's call with "Spencer for hire," which is a reference to the popular late-80s TV show Spenser: For Hire and also a nod to William Rabkin , cowriter of S1E9 " Forget Me Not " and author of five Psych novels, whose very first TV writing credit was for that show. Several mentions are made of CCP's San Antonio branch, and at one point Shawn mentions the city's Spurs NBA franchise. James Roday Rodriguez was born and raised in San Antonio and is a Spurs fan.
